content.subtitle: Use
rows: To convey what someone wanted to know
'**Did** you **finish** the report?' she asked him. → She asked him **if** he **had finished** the report.

rows.type: questions beginning with have, do or be

examples.direct: '**Have** you **met** her before?' he asked Tom.
examples.reported: He asked Tom **if** he **had met** her before.

examples.direct: '**Are** you **coming** to the party?' I asked Ben.
examples.reported: I asked Ben **if** he **was coming** to the party.

examples.direct: '**Can** you cook well?' she asked her friend.
examples.reported: She asked her friend **if** he **could** cook well.

examples.direct: '**Will** you lend me your notes?' I asked Karen.
examples.reported: I asked Karen **if** she **would** lend me her notes.

examples.direct: '**Shall** I bring something?' I asked Mum.
examples.reported: I asked Mum **if** I **should** bring something.

examples.direct: '**May** I use your phone?' Tim asked me.
examples.reported: Tim asked me **if** he **might** use my phone.

rows.type: questions beginning with a question word

examples.direct: '**What** time does the train arrive?' my sister asked me.
examples.reported: My sister asked me **what** time the train **arrived**.

examples.direct: '**Who** did you speak to?' asked Lisa.
'**Which** bus do you take?' Jack asked Sara.
'**When** will the results come out?' I asked.
examples.reported: I asked **when** the results **would** come out.

examples.direct: '**Why** are you upset?' my dad asked me.
examples.reported: My dad asked me **why** I **was** upset.

examples.direct: '**How long** did the journey take?' Sue asked Tom.
examples.reported: Sue asked Tom **how long** the journey **had taken**.
The teacher **told** us **not to be** late.

examples.direct: 'Will you help me move this table?' I asked Phil.
examples.reported: I **asked** Phil **to help** me move the table.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
items: In reported questions, **whether** can be used as an alternative to **if**.
She asked him whether he had finished the report.

content.subtitle: Be careful
items: Do not use question word order in reported questions.

items.correct: She asked where I had parked.

items.incorrect: She asked where had I parked.

sections.title: A: Reported yes/no questions
sections.instructions: Rewrite the direct questions as reported questions using if.

items.sentence: "Are you feeling better?" the nurse asked me.
items.answer: The nurse asked me if I was feeling better.

items.sentence: "Have you sent the email?" Liam asked.
items.answer: Liam asked if I had sent the email.

items.sentence: "Can you speak Italian?" Sara asked Tom.
items.answer: Sara asked Tom if he could speak Italian.

items.sentence: "Will they arrive tonight?" Mum asked.
items.answer: Mum asked if they would arrive that night.

items.sentence: "Should we wait outside?" Daniel asked.
items.answer: Daniel asked if they should wait outside.

items.sentence: "Did you lock the door?" Dad asked me.
items.answer: Dad asked me if I had locked the door.

sections.title: B: Reported wh- questions
sections.instructions: Complete each reported question with the correct form.

items.sentence: "Where does your cousin work?" he asked. → He asked where my cousin ...... .
"Why are they laughing?" she asked. → She asked why they ...... laughing.
"When will the concert finish?" I asked. → I asked when the concert ...... .
"Who did you invite?" Emma asked. → Emma asked who I ...... .
"How long did the film last?" Tom asked. → Tom asked how long the film ...... .
"Which colour do you prefer?" Mia asked. → Mia asked which colour I ...... .
"Open your books," the teacher said to us.
items.answer: The teacher told us to open our books.

items.sentence: "Don't park here," the police officer said to me.
items.answer: The police officer told me not to park there.

items.sentence: "Please carry this bag," Grandma said to Leo.
items.answer: Grandma asked Leo to carry that bag.

items.sentence: "Please don't make any noise," the librarian said to the children.
items.answer: The librarian asked the children not to make any noise.

items.sentence: "Bring me the receipt," the assistant said to Anna.
items.answer: The assistant told Anna to bring him the receipt.

items.sentence: "Please don't eat my sandwich," Mark said to me.
items.answer: Mark asked me not to eat his sandwich.

items.sentence: "Do you live nearby?" he asked me. He asked me ...... nearby.
"Where did you put the keys?" she asked. She asked ...... the keys.
"Please wait here," the receptionist said. The receptionist ...... there.
"Don't touch the screen," Dad said. Dad told us ...... the screen.
"Can you open the window?" she asked. She asked me if I ...... open the window.
"When will the shop close?" he asked. He asked when the shop ...... .

sections.title: E: Key word transformation
sections.instructions: Complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning.

items.sentence:
"Do you play tennis?" she asked me. **IF**
She asked me ...... tennis.

"Where did you find that wallet?" Tom asked. **WHERE**
Tom asked ...... that wallet.

"Please text me later," Amy said to Ben. **ASKED**
Amy ...... later.

"Don't shout in class," the teacher said. **TOLD**
The teacher ...... in class.

"When are you moving house?" I asked. **WHEN**
I asked ...... house.

"Can I use your charger?" Lucy asked me. **WHETHER**
Lucy asked me ...... my charger.
